Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v3

Event Name

Making Krishna Happy

Event Date

24 Aug 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Govinda Sundara das

Completed

28 Dec 2020

Source Audio

http://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-069-Making%20Krishna%20Happy%20(Mumbai)-SB_8_7_5_6-dt_90_08_24.mp3

Source Video

e.g.

Verse

SB 8.7.4-8

Places

Bhadrikasram, Badrinath

Pastimes

Churning of Milk Ocean, Lord Ganesh circumambulating the whole universe

Persons

Lord Narayana, Lord Kurma, Lord Ajitha,Vasuki, Demons, Demigods,Lord Ganesh, Lord Karthikeya, Lord Siva

Themes

Overcoming Obstacles, becoming fearless by fully surrendering to Krishn’a will, Significance of appearance of Lord Ganesh

Lecture Transcription

Om namo bhagavate vasudevaya

iti tūṣṇīṁ sthitān daityān
vilokya puruṣottamaḥ
smayamāno visṛjyāgraṁ
pucchaṁ jagrāha sāmaraḥ

[SB 8.7.4]

Thus the demons remained silent, opposing the desire of the demigods. Seeing the demons and understanding their motive, the Personality of Godhead smiled. Without discussion, He immediately accepted their proposal by grasping the tail of the snake, and the demigods followed Him.

kṛta-sthāna-vibhāgās ta
evaṁ kaśyapa-nandanāḥ
mamanthuḥ paramaṁ yattā
amṛtārthaṁ payo-nidhim

[SB 8.7.5]

[After thus adjusting how the snake was to be held, the sons of Kaśyapa, both demigods and demons, began their activities, desiring to get nectar by churning the Ocean of Milk.]

mathyamāne ’rṇave so ’drir
anādhāro hy apo ’viśat
dhriyamāṇo ’pi balibhir
gauravāt pāṇḍu-nandana

[SB 8.7.6]

[O son of the Pāṇḍu dynasty, when Mandara Mountain was thus being used as a churning rod in the Ocean of Milk, it had no support, and therefore although held by the strong hands of the demigods and demons, it sank into the water.]

te sunirviṇṇa-manasaḥ
parimlāna-mukha-śriyaḥ
āsan sva-pauruṣe naṣṭe
daivenātibalīyasā

[SB 8.7.7]

[Because the mountain had been sunk by the strength of providence, the demigods and demons were disappointed, and their faces seemed to shrivel.]

vilokya vighneśa-vidhiṁ tadeśvaro
duranta-vīryo ’vitathābhisandhiḥ
kṛtvā vapuḥ kacchapam adbhutam mahat
praviśya toyaṁ girim ujjahāra

[SB 8.7.8]

[Seeing the situation that had been created by the will of the Supreme, the unlimitedly powerful Lord, whose determination is infallible, took the wonderful shape of a tortoise, entered the water, and lifted the great Mandara Mountain.]

Purport by his Divine Grace A.C Bhakti Vedanta Swami Prabhupada:

Here is evidence that the Supreme Personality of Godhead is the supreme controller of everything. As we have previously described, there are two classes of men — the demons and the demigods — but neither of them are supremely powerful. Everyone has experienced that hindrances are imposed upon us by the supreme power. The demons regard these hindrances as mere accidents or chance, but devotees accept them to be acts of the supreme ruler. When faced with hindrances, therefore, devotees pray to the Lord. Tat te ’nukampāṁ susamīkṣamāṇo bhuṣjāna evātma-kṛtaṁ vipākam. Devotees endure hindrances, accepting them to be caused by the Supreme Personality of Godhead and regarding them as benedictions. Demons, however, being unable to understand the supreme controller, regard such hindrances as accidental. Here, of course, the Supreme Personality of Godhead was present personally. It was by His will that there were hindrances, and by His will those hindrances were removed. The Lord appeared as a tortoise to support the great mountain. Kṣitir iha vipulatare tava tiṣṭhati pṛṣṭhe. The Lord held the great mountain on His back. Keśava dhṛta-kūrma-śarīra jaya jagadīśa hare. Dangers can be created by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and they can also be removed by Him. This is known to devotees, but demons cannot understand it.

Translation:

Seeing the situation that had been created by the will of the Supreme, the unlimitedly powerful Lord, whose determination is infallible, took the wonderful shape of a tortoise, entered the water, and lifted the great Mandara Mountain.

mahātmānas tu māṁ pārtha
daivīṁ prakṛtim āśritāḥ
bhajanty ananya-manaso
jṣātvā bhūtādim avyayam

[BG 9.13]

[O son of Pṛthā, those who are not deluded, the great souls, are under the protection of the divine nature. They are fully engaged in devotional service because they know Me as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, original and inexhaustible.]

That the great souls, the Mahatmas - they are always under the influence of the divine energy of the Lord. They are constantly being protected by the loving hands of the lord in every circumstance of life. Therefore, with this faith they have full confidence that nothing is happening by chance, nothing is happening by accident but everything is being orchestrated by the Supreme personality of Godhead simply, to facilitate them the opportunity of deepening their attachment, deepening their surrender at his lotus feet.

Krishna also says:

mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ
sūyate sa-carācaram
hetunānena kaunteya
jagad viparivartate

[BG 9.10]

[This material nature, which is one of My energies, is working under My direction, O son of Kuntī, producing all moving and nonmoving beings. Under its rule this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again.]

That, for a materialist who is acting independent of the will of God, who has left the divine grace of the Lord. Krishna is also the supreme controller of everything taking place with in such a person’s life, because the material nature which are being constantly activated by the three modes of nature is working at every moment under his supervision. So therefore, in all circumstances of life, nothing happens by chance but everything is taking place by the arrangement of Sri Krishna.

Here we find a wonderful example of the difference between the demigods and the demons. The demigods and the demons are here side by side, working together in a united manner for the purpose of churning the ocean of milk in order to obtain nectar. Of course, the demigods are sure that they will get the nectar and the demons are sure that they will get every drop. So actually, in their hearts there is absolutely no unity. There is simply enmity, hate, envy and a very very fierce feeling of competition. But externally, because they are both thinking that their own motives will be served, they have befriended one another and they are unified and working together. Of course, in the material world we find so often in politics, in business there is this superficial type of unity, where actually people have no affection for one another whatsoever. But simply because they feel that there is some self-interest, even if it is meant to ultimately destroy the person you are unified with, they will accept this unity. People are so much motivated by their sense gratification.

So, the demons, they have agreed to the proposal of churning the ocean of milk, simply because they are more powerful than the demigods at that time. So, they are thinking - if we get this nectar, we will easily be able to take it all. What can the demigods do at this time to prevent us from drinking every drop? So, with this promise they are working very hard. Of course, in this world whatever we work for we have to accept there will be many hindrances. Whether we are working in the devotional service of the Supreme Personality of God-head or whether we are even working for some foolish attachments such as money or fame or something like that. There will be many impediments and this impediment is a great test, and the demons of course, and the demigods - they are perceiving these tests with completely different vision. The demons are thinking why is this happening to us, what material arrangement can we make to rescue the situation and the demigods they are thinking…aah.. Krishna has some plan; I may not understand what it is. Krishna is…the depth of Krishna’s intelligence is inconceivable to any jivatma’s mind but surely this is the divine will of the lord. Surely Krishna does have a very very...aah...illuminating gift that he wants to offer us through this situation. After all, all things work for the good for those who love God.

So therefore, a devotee even in the face of great crisis, he prays

tat te ’nukampāṁ susamīkṣamāṇo bhuṣjāna evātma-kṛtaṁ vipākam

[Srimad Bhagavatam 10.14.8]

[My dear Lord, one who earnestly waits for You to bestow Your causeless mercy upon him, all the while patiently suffering the reactions of his past misdeeds and offering You respectful obeisances with his heart, words and body, is surely eligible for liberation, for it has become his rightful claim.]

My dear Lord, thank you very much. You are giving me this suffering only by your causeless mercy. I deserve so much more but by your inconceivable will you are giving me just exactly what I need to help me turn my consciousness back to the shelter of your lotus feet and he faces the greatest hindrances, the greatest pains with folded hands and obeisance, thinking only of Krishna. When we have this faith in the truth that Krishna is the supreme controller, that he is the cause of all causes, then what anxiety can there be in our life. It is this phase that is the basis and the foundation of peace. Peace created by any material adjustments is not peace at all. It is the temporary relief from the natural condition of this violent world of birth and death. But, inevitably that condition of so-called peace must vanish right before our eyes. Things cannot always work out the way we want them. After all no one wants to grow old, get diseased and die but it is forced upon us. No one wants earthquakes but it is forced upon us, no one wants floods or heat waves or droughts. Hurricanes, cyclones, no one wants wars but they happen, unavoidably. Why is this? Because, Maya is very strong and we are insignificant and in this material world we are simply like the most insignificant insects before the powers of the demigods, before the powers of the modes of material nature.

So, all of our so-called arrangements for happiness and peace within society must be futile. To the degree we are taking shelter of those arrangements we are making, to that degree we are in illusion, we are in ignorance. To the degree we accept the fact that we are helpless and with faith in the Lord understand he is the controller, he is doing this for my good, therefore in this situation I should simply surrender to his lotus feet. On the practical level I may have to deal with the situation but in my heart, I am not thinking that my efforts are got to create any solution. My efforts are simply an offering of service to please him, but the solution is my surrendering to his divine will from the core of my heart.

When Krishna was speaking to Arjuna, Arjuna… the Pandavas they did not want to fight this war. It was inevitable. It came upon them beyond their control. They wanted to work together in unity with the Kurus. But even for devotees, things do not happen the way we want them. They tried by so many means to correct the situation and they were far more expert than any of us. But it was not possible. So, the war came upon them as a very very tremendous obstacle. Krishna did not tell them to fight and he did not tell them that you just fight and that will solve the problem. Nor did he tell them to give up the fight and that will solve the problem. He told them you just fight as a service to me. Your faith that ultimately, I am the controller and the result is whatever I desire, that will keep you peaceful throughout the fight. Then you will not be attached to success or failure, victory or defeat, honour or dishonour, gain or loss, simply fight for the sake of fighting. Imagine how much pressure was on Arjuna.

Yesterday someone was asking whether it’s good when there is too much pressure on us. It’s necessary; there must be pressure on us because without pressure our general tendency is, we become lazy, we become lethargic. But that pressure should not put us in anxiety. That pressure should rather simply motivate us to put every drop of our mind, every drop of our intelligence, every drop of our physical power with full enthusiasm in the Lord’s service, otherwise without pressure we will all just give half of our heart to Krishna, half of our heart to whatever we are doing. But our pressure is not to motivate us to attain a result; our pressure is to motivate us to execute our service. The result is in Krishna’s hands. Our service may be to strive for a result. That is always our service. Whatever we do we are striving for a result but we are not attached to the result, we are attached simply to the service of striving. Therefore, we don’t really care whether we win or lose, we really don’t care whether we are a success or a failure. We are simply concerned that I am pleasing Krishna by my endeavour. This is what Bhagavad Gita is teaching and when we develop this consciousness then we are Krishna conscious. But if we are aspiring for the fruits of our action, then we are simply caste into the ocean of the modes of material nature and the waves of those modes are constantly pushing us and beating upon us. Sometimes pushing us up, sometimes pushing us down. Hmm…the nature of the modes of material nature is… these laws are similar to the laws of gravity. What comes up must go down.

Yesterday someone was asking that he knows somebody who is one of the most pious and peaceful man he has ever met in his life. He said, this man is so calm under all circumstances, he is very pious, he is very charitable, he is always thinking good things to others, he never criticizes. Not only that but when his own father died, he was just very detached, he didn’t cry… he just said well what to do…there’s nothing we can do… let us just go on with our life and he has such qualities but he does not believe that there is a God. He believes that Rama and Krishna, that...they are... they are only stories and just like there are so many philosophers in the west, there are so many philosophers in the east and they just… you know according to what the society needs to make them think that everything is all right, they write some books…but actually there is no real absolute truth. So, he was saying, how is this that this person is so advanced, he appears to be liberated, he appears to have attained mukti and he does not even believe in God. You see… by the modes of material nature we become influenced by our previous karma. If one has acted very piously in the past, the results of his previous karma can be that a person is very peaceful, very sober, very happy, very equipoised in all circumstances, he may even be very charitable, very sympathetic toward others but he is not liberated. He is simply reaping the results of his past karma. But the law of karma is the law of nature just as the law of gravity is the law of nature, which means what goes up must come down. So now he is up but how long can he stay up. Inevitably when his good karma runs out, when it is exhausted, then his consciousness will dive down into the modes of passion and ignorance, and then he will be frustrated, he will be in anxiety, he will begin to engage in sinful activities. If he does not elevate himself to the transcendental state from the sattva guna, then it’s just a matter of time till he must be pushed down again because the modes of material nature like waves in the ocean… sometimes they push you up and then the next wave comes down and pushes you under.

So, this is not the liberated state. It is like building a palace on sand. The palace looks wonderful but when the storm comes it has to fall. So therefore, it is better if one is a sinful person, it is better if you are in the mode of ignorance addicted to intoxication, gambling, meat eating and illicit sex and you realize that I am a fallen soul and, in that state, you cry out for the Lord - Please save me please help me than being in the so-called mode of goodness - very pious, very charitable, very peaceful and being so puffed up, so proud that you think you can judge the position of God. You are in such a high level that you are categorizing the position of Veda Vyas, Narada muni, Ramanujacharya, Madhavacharya, thinking that your opinion is the final judgment of who these people really are...Haan... This man was saying but this person seems so humble. I said, so humble!!... What kind of humility is this? He may be humble in his daily activities. That’s just an appearance but in his heart, he thinks that he is so great that he could make judgmental statements to categorize the position of God and all of his incarnations, and all of the great saints. He is thinking his opinion is the final. It’s better to be on the bottom on the way up than on the top on the way down…Haan….

Srila Prabhupada has said that there are many sanyasis in India who have never seen in their life, who follow all the principles of sannyas strictly… the external principles, who know the Vedas very scrutinizingly… externally, but who are so proud they cannot put their head at the feet of a great devotee and take shelter of the lord. They think that they are very learned and great. He said he has come to America and he has found these hippies addicted to every sinful activity but they’ll very rarely admit that I am fallen, I am degraded, I am sinful please help me and they will put their feet, they will put their head at the feet of a pure soul and beg him, please give me your mercy. Srila Prabhupada said the hippies of the west are more advanced in spiritual life than even the great sannyasis of India because they are humble, because they accept that they are sinful, because they accept that they need the shelter of God and they are willing to cry out and then the same man we offered him a book. Take this book, read it...he said, “No no I already know these books, I already know everything that’s in these books, I have read all the books, I know the Vedas”. Ahh…You know the Vedas? Oh you know what it says in the Vedas? In the Vedas it says that one who says he knows does not know and one, who says he does not know, knows… so what is your position. So, he became very quiet.

So, you see…haan… by our material calculations, by our material arrangements there is no solution. We can at the best create a temporary solution to the problems but actually that’s worse. Because when you create a temporary solution which creates some peace in your life then you become attached to that. Then when that’s taken away, you suffer more than if you never had that peace, right? When everything’s always bad you get used to it and you think, ‘All right, I just have to accept it’, even if you are a materialist you just have to accept it. When everything is bad and then you are by your manipulation of material energy, you make things very good and you become attached to the good then when it gets back to the bad state, that’s more painful than death. So, all our material arrangements cannot possibly make us more peaceful or happy in the long run. They will make us more miserable. The more successful we are, ultimately the more miserable we will become. To never have anything is painful but to have it and lose it is thousands of time more excruciating to one’s heart. Isn’t it not? It’s like the poor people… they want more but this is my lot, this is the way I always been. They don’t mind so much. But, somebody that’s very rich they loses everything they become in the same position, and they cannot even live, they cannot even survive, they are in so much anxiety, they are so much depending on the facilities that they have created for themselves. So therefore, there is no solution in any way, shape or form by our material manipulations to try find happiness. In the…in the face of eternity whatever solution we make is only so temporary, so flickering and it inevitably will change again by the forces of the modes of material nature. What goes up must come down. That is the law of gravity, that is the law of karma.

ye hi saṁsparśa-jā bhogā
duḥkha-yonaya eva te
ādy-antavantaḥ kaunteya
na teṣu ramate budhaḥ

[BG 5.22]

[An intelligent person does not take part in the sources of misery, which are due to contact with the material senses. O son of Kuntī, such pleasures have a beginning and an end, and so the wise man does not delight in them.]

An intelligent man does not strive for such material pleasures, for such material prosperity because he knows it has a beginning and an end and it’s a source of misery, even his success is a source of misery. What tastes like nectar in the beginning tastes like poison in the end. That is the words of Krishna. Therefore, there is only one solution. As Vallabba Acharya prays - Sri Krishna Saranam Mama - to take shelter of the lotus feet of Krishna. That is the only solution.

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja

[BG 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

Forget about all these manipulative endeavours, even in the name of religion, just surrender unto me Krishna says - “I shall relieve you of all sinful reactions, do not fear”. Our anxiety is due to fear. When we take shelter at the feet of God there is nothing to fear. Therefore, the only condition of peace is faith. Faith that Krishna is good, that he is the controller, that not a blade of grass can move without his sanction, that everything that’s happening is his loving mercy toward me. After all being a devotee and striving in devotional service, we are not any longer simply under the modes of material nature. It may appear that we are, but they are being manipulated by Krishna, for our good even if we commit a sinful activity and there is a reaction. It’s not our Karma; Krishna is personally slapping us with his divine lotus hand just to try to wake us up, just to try to sober us from our stupor of intoxicated conditioning. Don’t you see what you are doing to your life… tishhh… Like a father, if a child is acting crazy, sometimes the father or mother has to slap the child…’wake up you crazy fool don’t you see what you are doing’… ‘Ah yes, I have done wrong…haan… all of our pains, all of our sufferings - Krishna is trying to purify us, Krishna is trying to give us the opportunity that you never have to take birth in this material world again.

When one comes into the divine movement of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he is not meant to ever take birth in this material world again. Therefore, Krishna will do everything within his power to make this our last birth, to send us back to Godhead. But it is not easy until we genuinely from the core of our heart cry out - ‘Krishna I am yours, from this day on I am yours, nothing is mine, I am not mine, I am yours, everything is yours’. Srila Prabhupada explained, that if you can one time with a heart full of devotion and sincerity cry out - ‘Krishna from this day on I am yours’. At that movement you are liberated in pure bhakti. Krishna will pick you up and take you back to Godhead from your heart. He will reveal the truth at once. But, can we say that with sincerity, that Krishna! From this day on, I am yours. What does that mean – ‘I am yours’.

Lord Chaitanya explained in Siksashtakam - āśliṣya vā pāda-ratāḿ…that if you want, Krishna! Embrace me, if you want trample upon me, if you want make me broken hearted by your absence, you are free to deal with me in any way you want, I am simply your servant unconditionally, and that’s all I want is your service unconditionally.

nāhaṁ vipro na ca nara-patir nāpi vaiśyo na śūdro

nāhaṁ varṇī na ca gṛha-patir no vanastho yatir vā

kintu prodyan-nikhila-paramānanda-pūrnāmṛtābdher

gopī-bhartuḥ pada-kamalayor dāsa-dāsānudāsaḥ

[CC Madhya 13.80]

[I am not a brāhmaṇa, I am not a kṣatriya, I am not a vaiśya or a śūdra. Nor am I a brahmacārī, a householder, a vānaprastha or a sannyāsī. I identify Myself only as the servant of the servant of the servant of the lotus feet of Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the maintainer of the gopīs. He is like an ocean of nectar, and He is the cause of universal transcendental bliss. He is always existing with brilliance.]

My Lord, I am not a brahmana or a kshatriya or a vaishya or a Sudra, I am not a brahmacari, grihastha, vanaprastha or sannyasi. These are all external designations. My only desire, my only hope, my only position striving in life is just to be the servant of the servant of the servant of the servant of your servant. The servant does not demand from the master. The servant simply performs his service and accepts whatever the master provides as a result. That is a real servant. The very unique glory of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu’s followers, that they pray Krishna I am giving you everything, I am doing everything for you and in return I expect nothing. Others are praying to God, I will do this tapasya, I will do this austerity, give me something in return but a devotee performs the greatest tapasya of surrendering to the mission of the lord whatever it is and expects nothing in return, asks for nothing in return. Krishna! All I want is the right to serve, all I want is the opportunity to please you.

na dhanaḿ na janaḿ na sundarīḿ
kavitāḿ vā jagad-īśa kāmaye
mama janmani janmanīśvare
bhavatād bhaktir ahaitukī tvayi

[Sikshastakam verse 4]

[O Lord of the universe, I do not desire material wealth, materialistic followers, a beautiful wife or fruitive activities described in flowery language. All I want, life after life, is unmotivated devotional service to You.]

Seeing in his ecstasy because liberated, can’t control their….can’t control themselves from praising the lord. Like a mad man, no hard and fast rules. Lord Chaitanya is praying - I do not want money, I do not want beautiful woman surrounding me, I do not want fame, position, popularity, I do not even want liberation, I do not even want freedom from pain, suffering and anxiety, I only want one thing - that I may please you by engaging in your service birth after birth after birth. This is the lesson of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, that his devotees… they simply want the opportunity to serve and whatever condition is put upon us, however long and treacherous it may seem... there is absolutely no question of anxiety if we simply have faith that Krishna is in my heart. He knows exactly what the situation is and he knows what’s best, and he is providing what is best. I will act according to his desire, but there is no anxiety. I will fight, I will work according to the guidance of my Guru Maharaj, but the results are in Krishna’s hands.

When Krishna sent his little cowherd boyfriends to see the brahmans to beg for charity, they accepted the mission of the lord. They were not begging for themselves. They went to the brahmans to beg for food, for Prasad so that they can bring it back to Krishna and Balaramji, and the brahmans totally ignored them, which is a great insult. If someone calls you a bad name, that’s an insult but if someone totally ignores you and doesn’t even acknowledge your existence, that’s far more of an insult. That means he doesn’t even give you enough credit to even say anything to you. The greatest insult is being totally ignored. They were totally ignored; they were begging like humble beggars.

The brahmans did not even look upon them and the boys became very dejected. They went to Krishna... Krishna! They would not give us anything. Krishna said, “Why are you feeling bad? You did your service… that is the joy of the soul, to perform the service. The results - that is not your concern, that is my concern, my business is the results, don’t try to interfere with my business. Your business is the service, your business is to try to get the results and if you are trying to get the results, your life is perfect. That is the success in and of itself. Whether I give or don’t give, that is not your concern. You should not be attached to the fruits of your actions; you should simply be attached to the service, for the pleasure of the Lord”.

The Lord does not need our results. He already possesses everything. When you offer water to Mother Ganga, does mother Ganga need your water? She is giving you the water to offer. She does not need the water, but she enjoys and takes great pleasure in your offering. So, Krishna enjoys… he takes pleasure in our bhakti, in our devotion, in our service attitude. Without a proper service attitude, nothing within our consciousness is pleasing to Krishna. Not even the chanting of the holy name. Some people ask why is it that the people sometimes…they chant… they apparently chant very… with great discipline for ten, twenty years and still they have so many bad qualities. How is this possible? Krishna is not concerned with our chanting. He is concerned with how we are chanting. He is not concerned with the number of our mantras. He is concerned with the quality of how we are offering these mantras. In other words, Krishna is independent, he is the supreme autocrat. If we chant his name he doesn’t have to appear with in his name. If he wants the name it will just be a material sound vibration without potency. The degree of potency is to the degree Krishna decides he wants to appear within his name and he reciprocates with how menial our service attitude is. If we are chanting in this attitude of the menial servant then Krishna will fully manifest himself within his name. But, if we are proud and we have no service attitude and we chant the holy names, that sound vibration is simply like a shadow of Krishna’s presence.

Therefore, how did Srila Prabhupada teach us to chant? He told us 99% of our spiritual advancement is how we are is by chanting 16 rounds and having kirtan - The chanting of the holy name. 99% of our spiritual advancement is…is in our chanting of the holy name… haan… You have heard that. So then why is it we chant japa for an hour and half, may be two hours every day and kirtan at the most two hours (38.58,59-Audio Unclear) we allow to sleep six hours and is it we have 18 hours, that means fourteen hours you are doing other activities and at the most four hours you are chanting the holy names. So, 99% of our advancement is chanting the holy names. Why don’t we just chant all day? Why we are spending 14 hours sweeping floors, distributing books, managing businesses, executing our profession, cooking food…haan… dealing with management, getting permits from the government...haan…what is this have to do with… why don’t I just go to Vrindavan I chant all day. If 99% of my advancement is chanting, why should I do all this other crazy things? Why fourteen hours of these things and three or four chanting.

The reason is all these other activities are devotional service, other…all these other activities are meant to be done in the spirit of obedient, menial service to the Guru, and by that fourteen hours of menially serving the Guru, you develop a proper attitude that I am the servant, I am the humble servant, I am the insignificant servant and you develop that attitude of what can I offer to you? How can I serve you and when you chant the holy name in that spirit of being a servant, then your chanting has real quality, has divine quality.

Therefore, to go to Vrindavan to be a Babaji before you are completely immersed in a total attitude of being a menial servant is not productive at all for spiritual life. You could chant 64 rounds, you could chant 3 Lakhs of rounds, but it will not be as pleasing to Krishna if you are thinking I am advanced, I am proud, I am babaji, I am great sadhu, I am taking shelter of the dham, just see how nice I am. If we don’t have the servant attitude, the obedient, menial, meniality that, “Guru Maharaj, whatever you want I will do,” If you do not have that feeling… Someone who is chanting 16 rounds and…and the rest of the day he is just being harassed by all sorts of difficulties that he has to endure on behalf of his Guru in the middle of a horrible city like Bombay, such a person is going to make millions of times more advancement than a person who is chanting all day long without that service attitude.

Therefore, great souls like Haridas Thakur, they were totally immersed in the service attitude twenty-four hours a day. So, when they would chant three lakhs of the names of Krishna every day, every name was crying out to Krishna, ‘how can I serve you, how can I please you’ and this is exactly what Krishna wanted him to do, this is exactly what his Guru Maharaj wanted him to do. You chant all day, and purify the whole universe by the glory of your pure chanting. So, the quality is what is necessary and this is what Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and the great acharyas are teaching us how to chant in the proper quality of mind – ‘I am your servant,’ and how to prepare ourselves every day. We should be thinking all day long. Whatever we do, the real purity of our activities is to the degree we are adopting the state of mind that I am the menial, obedient servant. I am not demanding, I am not requesting, I am simply trying to please and, in this way, we are preparing ourselves to chant the holy names perfectly and make real spiritual advancement by the recitation of the Maha Mantra. If you have faith that Krishna is perfect and he is in control, then what is there to fear, what is the anxiety? Krishna’s plan is good and it is only when we develop that consciousness, then there can be real peace.

brahma-bhūtaḥ prasannātmā
na śocati na kāṅkṣati
samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu
mad-bhaktiṁ labhate parām

[BG 18.54]

[One who is thus transcendentally situated at once realizes the Supreme Brahman and becomes fully joyful. He never laments or desires to have anything. He is equally disposed toward every living entity. In that state he attains pure devotional service unto Me.]

When one attains the spiritual platform of life, he does not hanker for anything nor lament for anything. He accepts Krishna’s mercy in whatever way it comes and he is grateful.

So here we find the demigods and the demons, they are the most powerful controllers in all the universe. You know how much control the demigods have. They are controlling the rains, the wind, they are controlling the laws of gravity, they are controlling the fire, they are controlling the movement of the planets. Who are we compared to that, and the demons - they were so powerful that they conquered the demigods and took… headed by Bali Maharaj… they took all the acquisitions and all the responsibilities away from the demigods and the demons became the controllers of the whole universe. So, this class of men that we are reading about in Bhagavatam are just inconceivably, uncomprehensibly powerful. Compared to them we are not even insects and yet, in trying to execute their duty, to fulfil their desires, to move this Mandara mountain and to churn the ocean of milk with it, at every step they are being baffled, every step they are confronting a situation which is beyond their control, beyond their power.

First, they make a truce and are told, take this mountain of gold and bring it to the ocean of milk. ‘yes yes yes’… that mountain of gold was so heavy that as they were trying to move it, they were falling under it, they were screaming and crying, it was so heavy their fingers were bleeding…(45.35 to 44-Audio Unclear) as it was falling, it was crushing their fingers, crushing their arms, crushing their limbs. Most of the demigods and demons were being crushed to death under the weight of this mountain. They were howling and screaming and crying, “we can’t do it”, and yet they kept trying and trying of no avail. Ultimately Lord Narayan had to come. You think you have the power to do anything… I don’t have power you cannot do anything. Lord Narayan with his one hand, he effortlessly picked up the mountain, put it on the back of Garuda and told all the demigods and demons you all get on Garuda with me. They all boarded Garuda and he took them all to the ocean of milk. Garuda was not tired, he was not fatigued, he was just going. Garuda is the servant but because he was empowered by Lord Narayan, he acted even more powerfully than Narayan. Narayan effortlessly lifted the mountain but Garuda not only took the mountain but all the demigods and all the demons and they were big, powerful personalities. They were millions of them and he took them all to the ocean of milk without in anyway feeling tired. He wasn’t straining. So, Krishna’s inconceivable power is he can empower you with even more strength than himself if he wants. But whether you are Garuda or whether you are the demigods or the demons, you are completely dependent on Krishna’s mercy. You cannot do anything, you cannot even lift your finger without his help, you cannot even open up your eyes without his help.

prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāniguṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśah

[BG 3.27]

[The spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature.]

We are thinking, “oh I am the doer, I am very good”. We can do nothing without Krishna, and then they start churning. Finally, by the grace of God the mountain is in the ocean of milk. They get Vasuki. They start churning the ocean and now what happens? The ocean is…the mountain starts sinking into the ocean and they are trying to keep it up and they cannot keep it up with all their power, with all their efforts… cannot keep it up. Do you know what it is when such a big big powerful controller is a failure, you know how anxiety they get in…haan… someone like me… you know can’t do anything right. When we fail, we just think well that’s the way it always happens but when the demigods and the demons, they were conquerors of everything. Everything is always act… working just the way they want it. When they miserably failing, my God! They are frustrated; they don’t know what to do. They were struggling, struggling, struggling… they could not keep the mountain. It was sinking lower and lower and lower and lower. Finally, again, Krishna appeared in the form of Kurma Rupa.

kshitir iha vipulatare tishthati tava prishthe
dharani-dharana-kina-cakra-garishthe
kesava dhrita-kurma-sarira jaya jagadisa hare

[Sri Dasavatara Stotra by Jayadeva Goswami - verse 2]

[0 Kesava! O Lord of the universe! O Lord Hari, who have assumed the form of a tortoise! All glories to You! In this incarnation as a divine tortoise the great Mandara Mountain rests upon Your gigantic back as a pivot for churning the ocean of milk. From holding up the huge mountain a large scarlike depression is put in Your back, which has become most glorious.]

The Lord appeared as a big big turtle. His back was literally hundreds and thousands of miles in length and width. When the Lord wants to be a turtle, he is not like an ordinary turtle. One may ask how is it possible that a tortoise can be so big. Acintya Shakti …Krishna can do anything at any time. There is nothing impossible for the lord. So, he took the form of this gigantic, wonderful tortoise and very gracefully as tortoises move, he crawled under the mountain, put the mountain on his back and his shell became the support and the pivot for the mountain, to hold it in place and then they began to churn and then what happens is the top of the mountain started swaying back and forth. We are going to read this and as they were swaying back and forth, they were thinking my God what to do, how we are going to churn it, we have to keep the top in place and everything within their effort they could not keep it in place. Again they were total failures - the supreme controllers of the universe, they could do nothing, they were helpless. They could not execute their duty.

Kurma Rupa was holding it from the bottom and then he appeared as Ajita and he appeared on the top of the mountain and with his hand he held it. (50.4 - word not clear) steady… haan ok… now we can start to churn, now we will churn very nicely. Then they started to churn, and what happened is that as they started to churn, they couldn’t move the mountain. It was so heavy…you know you have to make it go back and forth. They were pulling on the serpent vasuki with all their might, going back and forth and they could hardly get it to move, what to speak of fast enough to churn the ocean, and they were so frustrated, they were crying out as they were pulling with all their might, all their strength. They couldn’t do it. Finally, Krishna again he incarnated in the mode of goodness and filled the demigods with his empowerment because you see the demigods get strength from the mode of goodness and then through the mode of passion and ignorance, he incarnated and filled the demons because that’s what… it is passion and ignorance that is the motivating force, it gives strength to the demons.

So, in this way Krishna empowered to give them strength, they didn’t have it. Then they started to churn and it started to move…Tchu tchu tchu… Then what happens… then Vasuki, he is getting so hot because they are churning so fast that fire starts pouring out of his mouth and all the demons were getting burnt. We have read yesterday that Krishna took the mouth of Vasuki because he knew this would happen, he wanted to protect them but the demons didn’t trust Krishna. The demon is thinking everyone is… must be like me. I am envious and I am only concerned with number one and I will only take the position that is good for me, so therefore Krishna must be the same way. So, he is taking the head because the head is the best place. Actually, Krishna was taking the head to protect them. But because they were so envious, they are thinking everyone is envious of me. Krishna is trying to cheat me because I am a cheater, everyone is a cheater. So, they said, “No no, we will not churn this ocean unless we get the head.” Krishna said...he smiled… and you take the head, you crazy fellows and when he took the head and he started, the poisonous flames started pouring out, the demons were being burnt to ashes and even on the other side the demigods… they were so hot from these pouring flames… they didn’t know what to do. So again, they could not churn. They could not do their duty. So, then Krishna incarnated as like rain and this rain cooled off Vasuki and cooled off everybody and again, they started to churn. So, understand that these great controllers, however big they might be in our eyes, they cannot do anything without the help of Krishna. Every stage of this Lila, they were helpless until Krishna came to help them…haan... kesava dhrita-kurma-sarira jaya jagadisa hare

This divine incarnation of Kurma Rupa is very important to understand, because whatever we do in life, we should understand that without the grace of Krishna it has no support. We are all concerned with security and support but everything - all of our security and all of our support is exclusively resting on the mercy of God and without his mercy nothing can be upheld…haan… So this great tortoise incarnation is showing us by his divine example that however great we are, even up to the point of the greatest personalities in the entire universe, we are completely depending on the support of the Supreme Personality of Godhead for our very existence or to get anything done…haan.. So when we think of Kurma Rupa, we should pray to him – ‘whatever I have it is only by your support that I can preserve it and whatever I do not have it is only by your support that I can ever attain it. It is only by your support that I can survive moment after moment.’

So, the incarnation of Kurma is the Lord’s incarnation which is exhibiting to the world how he is the supreme support for everything that exist and everything that we do and we are completely depending on his mercy at all times. This huge mountain… try to understand the difference between Krishna and the rest of us. This huge mountain that was crushing the limbs of all these demigods and demons and they were screaming and crying, when… even one part of them would fall upon them, the whole mountain was on his back and they were churning it, and it was grinding back and forth but Kurma Rupa was so mighty and so powerful that it was just like a relieving a scratching to the hitch on his back and he was feeling great pleasure…haan...that is the difference between he and us. He is great, God is great and we are insignificant. He is Vibhu which means unlimited, we are Anu which means so small that it’s not even worth talking about ourselves…haan… How can we…how can we find happiness talking about ourselves when we understand Krishna is Vibhu and we are Anu.

In the present…in the presence of a one crore rupee cheque, do you talk about…do you talk about the glories of one paisa? Aah… just see I have one paisa. If you have nothing else, you think…aah… one paisa is a lot. But when you have a cheque for one crore or cash… one crore rupees, you think...aah… I have one crore rupees, you don’t say I have one paisa…haan…in the presence of something great, you are never concerned with something tiny. In the presence of God who is unlimited, we are nothing. We are not even worth talking about, we are not even worth thinking about, we should just think of Krishna, we should just praise Krishna. That is our… that is…if we praise Krishna, the happiness from that…from that glorification would be as infinite as Krishna is but if we praise ourselves the happiness of that glorification will be as insignificant as we are…haan… So therefore, praise the Lord my friend, forget your own so-called glories. After all they are all borrowed plumes. It is only by the mercy of Kurma dev that we are having any support to do anything with in our life. So, all glories should go to him. kesava dhrita-kurma-sarira jaya jagadisa hare … that is our prayer.

Today is the day of Lord Ganesh… haan… So, people worship Ganesh. Om Ganesha pate namaha, they offer their obeisances to Lord Ganesh for the purpose of removing obstacles in our life. They pray to Lord Ganesh for the purpose of helping us to become successful in our endeavours. Is this the main reason people approach Lord Ganapati? In the Vedas it is described, these are his two functions in this universe - He removes obstacles from our endeavours and he bestows us with success in our efforts. Now, in the Bhakti Rasamrita Sindhu, Srila Rupa Goswami explains that before beginning our Deity worship or our puja, it is conducive to bhakti to offer our obeisances to Ganesh. Om Ganesh Pate Namaha… that is the mantra he gives. But our desire is not material. We understand Ganesh, your service is to remove obstacles, so please remove obstacles in my service to Lord Hari.

Ultimately Krishna says in Bhagavad-gita, whatever the demigods do, they are only the distributors of my mercy. I am providing… just like if Sri Dayananda decides I am not going to sell any Mafatlal fine products to a particular store that’s exclusively selling his things to the public, what will happen to that store? Will they have anything if it is not give them… they cannot give to you, they are out of business, they are finished. So, if Krishna does not give the demigods the benedictions, the facilities for those benedictions, all the demigods would go bankrupt…haan… they have no business, they will have nothing to give, no one will come to them anymore if they have nothing to give. They will lose all their customers.

So, Krishna is providing everything to everyone, but these devotees are very very special, very very dear. Ganesh is not an ordinary demigod. He is directly the son of Shankar, he is directly the son of Lord Shiva, Vaisnavanam yatha Shambhu. Lord Shiva is the supreme-most almighty of all the demigods. He is not even a demigod. He is actually an expansion of Visnu himself. He plays the role of a demigods but he is beyond. He is not a jivatma. He is the supreme devotee of Godhead… and of course one who is the beloved son of such a great soul… imagine his exalted position. So, Lord Ganesh… he is the empowered child of Lord Shiva and he is given the glorious task of removing obstacles. So therefore, as devotees, we can worship Ganesh but if we really want to please him, we do not ask him – “Remove obstacles so that I can make a lot of money, because so that I can get a good husband or a wife, so that I can defeat my competitors, so that I can get good grades in school.” We should not ask a big man for something so small but unfortunately 99.9999% of devotees of Lord Ganesh, they are simply asking him for these petty little material benedictions.

Therefore -

kāmais tais tair hṛta-jṣānāḥ
prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ
taṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya
prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā

[BG 7.20]

[Those whose intelligence has been stolen by material desires surrender unto demigods and follow the particular rules and regulations of worship according to their own natures.]

Krishna says these persons are less intelligent, they are…they are simply motivated by lusty desires, persons who approach demigods for material benedictions. But we can offer all respect to that demigod factually by watering the root of the tree, all the tree is satisfied. If you satisfy your spiritual master who is offering that service to Radha Gopinath and Gopinath and Radharani are pleased, all the demigods are perfectly satisfied. We need not go to anyone else. But, you can also approach the demigods and if you approach them in such a way that - “my dear Lord, I do not want anything for my own sense gratification from you, but help me to improve my service to Lord Hari, remove the obstacles that are…that are creating…aah.. impediment within my heart in the service to my Guru Maharaj. If your goal is to please Krishna, then that is perfect.

So even great saints, even Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu himself would offer his obeisance’s and his prayers to Shankar, to Kartikeya, to Ganesh but he did it in this spirit to show us that yes, these are great souls, never offend them, never minimize them but understand what is their relationship to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and you can reciprocate and have a relationship with them in relationship to your devotional service to the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Ganesh is a very great devotee. You know the story of the race between Ganesh and Kartikeya…haan… it is a very famous story, everyone knows it. They were supposed to go around the world and Lord Kartikeya… he…he has a carrier. You know Kartikeya is very slender and very strong, he is a warrior. He really knows how to get around, he is very agile plus his carrier is a peacock and that peacock can just soar through the air at top speed and Ganesh…he is like a Pandit. He is not…You don’t see him so much in the battle field. Sometimes, but usually he is sitting up in Badrikashram, just writing the Vedas with Vyasadev…haan… and his devotees are always giving him all these laddus. So… he gets…he is overweight from material standards and he would ride on a rat… how fast is a rat… a mouse…haan… Mouses don’t fly so good.

So here it is a race between Kartikeya on his peacock and Ganesh on his rat. On your mark, get set, go… Kartikeya was off and Ganesh, he was like tutu tutu, tutu tutu… and he decided, “Well there is no use trying to win by this way.” So he just took to he wrote Ram on the ground and he just made a circle around the name of Ram…it took a second and within a few seconds later Kartikeya…Shhhhh… he is back…”hhaan...I am back… you are just still here.” Lord Shiva said, “haan, Ganesh won”…haan…because not only is the world within the name of Ram but the whole universe is within the name of Ram and he circumambulated the name of Ram. Therefore, he went around the whole universe one-tenth of the time that you went around the world. Not only the whole universe, he went around the whole existence. So, this is his faith. Ganesh has faith that the name of Ram is non-different than Ram and everything is contained within Ram…haan… So, he is a pure devotee. He is not an ordinary living being. He is the pure devotee of the Lord. This story proves that how much faith… infact Srila Prabhupada used to tell us that you become a pure devotee the day you have complete faith that Krishna is non-different than his name. When you have that complete faith that Krishna is absolutely non-different than his name, then you are a pure devotee. So, he exhibited this faith. So …very exalted position.

So, we can celebrate him for his devotion to Narayan. On his birthday we shouldn’t like people think…, ‘oh this is my opportunity to ask for the best thing…it’s his birthday. You ask him…if you ask him on his birthday then your special potency to your desire…haan… and he will reward.’ Just give up all this nonsense, lusty mentality. His birthday, we should glorify him because of his great devotion to the lord. Just like we’ll glorify any great Vaishnava. Such a great Vaishnava. So, we praise him for his special position as the Lord’s great devotee. We remember his great qualities, how he has complete faith in the holy name. How by his mercy, the Mahabharata and many other Vedic literatures are given to us. When we went to Badrikashram, you remember… we went to the cave of Veda Vyasa and about a fifteen minutes walk from that is the cave of Ganpati and Vyasdev sitting in that secluded place on the banks of the river Saraswati. Vyasa dev was reciting the Vedas and Ganapati, by his divine empowerment… of course elephants have big ears…he was able to hear from such a far away. It wasn’t that Vyasa dev was yelling, it’s not that he had a mouth amplifier to make a sound vibration, go wireless microphone, or a sound system… he was just talking - Om namo bhagavate vasudevaya… narayanam… janmādy asya yato. He was just speaking but because it was a spiritual transcendental sound through Ganesh’s ears, he was hearing the sound and then he was writing it down in beautiful Sanskrit handwriting and it was those pages he wrote which was given to the great saints and sages and became the guiding light for all the world, the sastra…haan… So, what a contribution he has given us, together with Vyasa dev…the two of them were empowered by Sri Krishna to provide all the Vedas, all the knowledge in which our lives are depending on. The Bhagavad-gita was written by Ganesh, it is part of Mahabharata.

So therefore, this wonderful service he has offered to the lord and this wonderful service he has offered to all of us. We are totally…we… we should be so obliged and thankful to him. So, we should praise him to glorify his service to Lord Hari. We shouldn’t be simply like… like beggars be asking - give me this, give me that. Such a great soul…we should be thinking, “What can I give you? You have given me the Vedas by the mercy of Krishna. You have given me an example of pure faith in the holy name, by the mercy of Krishna.”

So, the Vaishnavas - they understand the most pleasing and wonderful way of approaching these holy days –

ye yathā māṁ prapadyante tāṁs tathaiva bhajāmy aham

[BG 4.11]

[As all surrender unto Me, I reward them accordingly. Everyone follows My path in all respects, O son of Pṛthā.]

The gopis worship Durga to help them please Krishna. Rupa goswami taught us… because Durga, her function is to give husbands. The gopis knew that Krishna wanted them, it’s not that they wanted to be Krishna’s wives, is that they knew that Krishna wanted them to be his wives. Just like in Vrindavan there are two camps – there are Chandravali’s camp and Radharani’s camp and Radharani and Chandravali are always competing to try to please Krishna. It’s not that Radharani is against Chandravali, it’s not that she is thinking, “oh I am better than her and she is nonsense,” it’s not like this type of competition. She is not thinking that I want to enjoy Krishna instead of her. She is thinking that Krishna is the supreme enjoyer and Chadravali doesn’t really know how to please him properly. So therefore, I should do it, because I know what makes him happy. Devotees are always thinking in terms of what makes Krishna happy. So therefore, we should approach this day in such a way of understanding that my worship should be that worship which makes Krishna happy. That is my goal of life. On every holy day our prayer should be to that particular personality, to glorify them in their service to the Lord and to always be thinking how by your mercy I can improve the quality of my service, to give pleasure to Lord Krishna. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna!

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
